---
title: Taylor series
author: JK
date: '2020-03-31'
slug: taylor-series
categories:
  - Math
tags:
  - Analysis
  - Convex optimization
  - Machine learning
subtitle: ''
summary: ''
authors: []
lastmod: '2020-03-31T06:49:54+09:00'
featured: no
image:
  caption: ''
  focal_point: ''
  preview_only: no
projects: []
---

\[ f(x) = \sum_{k=0}^\infty c_k x^k = c_0 + c_1 x + c_2 x^2 + \dotsb. \] 


This is apporoximation which is function of h and derivatives of $f(x)$ are elements of parameters.  

$ f(x \pm h) = f(x) \pm hf'(x) + \frac{h^2}{2}f''(x) \pm \frac{h^3}{6}f^{(3)}(x) + O(h^4)$

Let's think about $\sin(x)$.  

\begin{align*} f(x) &= \sin(x) & f(0) &= 0
f'(x) &= \cos(x) & f'(0) &= 1
f(x) &= -\sin(x) & f(0) &= 0
f(x) &= -\cos(x) & f(0) &= -1
& \vdots \end{align*}

Thus,

\begin{align*} \sin(x) &= 0 + \frac{1}{1!}x + \frac{0}{2!}x^2 + \frac{-1}{3!}x^3 + \dotsb
&= x - \frac{x^3}{3!} + \frac{x^5}{5!} - \dotsb, \end{align*}


This is approximation. Now $x$ becomes $h$ and parameters calculated from derivatives of $f(x)$ at $0$.  
$f(x \pm h) = f(x) \pm hf'(x) + \frac{h^2}{2}f''(x) \pm \frac{h^3}{6}f^{(3)}(x) + O(h^4)$


![https://betterexplained.com/articles/taylor-series/](img/sine-better-models.png)
